creator: crees@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hey,

Thanks for your submission.

Please don't set PORTREVISION for new ports -- this also gives away that 
you haven't run portlint, or you've ignored the output!

CONFLICTS needs a version, and is probably unnecessary here since there 
isn't a dovecot1 related port AND if this depends on dovecot2 then 
there's no possibility of old dovecot being installed -- there's a 
conflict there.

You need a FETCH dependency on wget.

Please would you do something about hardcoding the paths in the patch?

Normally you want to do something like replacing /usr/lib/dovecot/imap 
with %%LOCALBASE%%/lib/dovecot in the patch, and then put in:

post-patch:
<tab>${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's/%%LOCALBASE%%/${LOCALBASE}/' ${WRKSRC}/Makefile

Actually, considering how simple the patch is, perhaps:

${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 
's,^\(DOVECOT_IMAP_PLUGIN_PATH\).*,\1=${LOCALBASE}/lib/dovecot,' \
     -e 's,^\(DOVECOT_INC_PATH\).*,\1=${LOCALBASE}/include/dovecot,' \
     ${WRKSRC}/Makefile

instead of the patchfile.

Also, considering the number of files, I'd probably use PLIST_FILES and 
PLIST_DIRS to save space/time/inodes.

Chris

--
